instruct: A male audiobook narrator in his late forties. Low-to-mid pitch with warm chest resonance, clean and full-bodied with no breathiness, rasp, or vocal fry. Neutral American accent, crisp consonants, fully articulated word endings. Reads at a steady unhurried pace with even energy across the phrase, narrow pitch variation, and a gentle downward inflection at each sentence end. Plain narration, not dramatised, no character voices. Recorded close to a large-diaphragm microphone in a dry, acoustically treated studio: no reverb, no room tone, no background noise or music.
ref_text: The distinction matters more than it first appears. What separates the two cases is not scale, or cost, or even timing, but who is left to absorb the consequences. Once that question is asked plainly, the earlier arguments begin to look like descriptions of the same problem.
